name: Shinseki v. Sanders
question: Did the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit err in presuming a prejudicial error when the VA fails to give notice to claimant as to who is responsible for obtaining evidence necessary to substantiate the claim?
description: A case in which the Court found that the lower court's framework for evaluating VA (Veterans' Affairs) notice errors conflicted with the established law that the Veterans Court "take due account of the rule of the prejudicial error."
